title: Living in Boulder vlog 7 - 12 - 2017: Cobble Stones of Rome

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief Boulder vlog update: his bike is at a shop getting pedal issues fixed, and while waiting to ride again he is working on a 3D or digital art project depicting the cobblestones of ancient Rome. He describes the project as relaxing 'brain candy' and mentions plans to eventually monetize his website.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Not that hard conceptually, but physically demanding when done on the bike; a good reserve activity
His computer is too underpowered to render the full project at once; expects better hardware in a couple of years
Getting back on the bike will provide physical accomplishment and free him up to pursue other goals like monetizing his website

entities: Ethan (historian, possibly a podcast or YouTube creator)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't do the video on the other day because I wasn't motivated and wanted to get going on my bike into my room.

claims.subject: Kyle's bike
claims.claim: I put pedals on my bike, but they hit the chain, so I took it to a bike shop on 18th and Pearl in Blacksburg.

claims.subject: bike shop
claims.claim: The bike shop said they could fix the pedals in three days, but I'm not sure about the cost.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm super excited to work on my project about the cobblestones of ancient Rome.

claims.subject: project
claims.claim: The cobblestones project is total brain candy and easy to do, serving as a reserve for bike cycling to get me started.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I gravitated to putting the cobblestones on while riding my bike the other day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The project is hard because I'm not in shape, but it will get easier as I get more fit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to make a demo video when I get my bike back.

claims.subject: project
claims.claim: I'm working on the plaza in front of the cobblestones, which is a large chunk of area, and then I'll fan out and do all the roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to Ethan this morning, who is into history, but now I'm watching Borges.

claims.subject: project
claims.claim: I think the project might be too big for my computer to handle all at once, so I'll do it in sections for YouTube videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In a couple of years, I might have a computer powerful enough to handle the whole project seamlessly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hope it won't take too long to program the rest of the road.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I ride my bike, it will be easier and more physical, giving me a sense of accomplishment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to work on the service level of my website and try to make sure my target is sold to start making money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to get back to working on Borges and my cobblestones project.

claims.subject: project
claims.claim: The cobblestones project has been going on for a long time, but it's good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to put the cobblestones on tiles too when I'm done, and it's going to be really cool.
